auto_approve.mechanism: Claude Code does not support pre-configured auto-approval for specific MCP tools; all tool calls require user confirmation unless covered by session permission rules

enterprise_management.mechanism: mcp_managed_config: managed-mcp.json for exclusive control, or allowedMcpServers/deniedMcpServers in managed settings for policy-based control

env_var_expansion.mechanism: mcp_env_var_expansion: ${VAR} and ${VAR:-default} syntax in command, args, env, url, and headers fields of .mcp.json

marketplace.mechanism: Claude Code does not have an in-IDE MCP marketplace for discovery and installation

oauth_support.mechanism: mcp_oauth_authentication: OAuth 2.0 for HTTP MCP servers; dynamic client registration or CIMD auto-discovery; token storage in macOS keychain or credentials file; auto-refresh; oauth.scopes pins requested scope set; authServerMetadataUrl overrides discovery chain

resource_referencing.mechanism: mcp_resources: MCP resources accessible via @server:protocol://path syntax; appear in @ autocomplete; auto-fetched when referenced

tool_filtering.mechanism: Claude Code exposes all tools from connected MCP servers; no per-server tool allowlist or blocklist for individual tool visibility

transport_types.mechanism: mcp_transport_types: stdio (local process), SSE (deprecated, still supported), HTTP/streamable-HTTP (recommended for remote)

providerExtensions.name: Three Transport Types (stdio, SSE, HTTP)
providerExtensions.summary: Three transport types: stdio (local process), SSE (deprecated), HTTP/streamable-HTTP (recommended for remote with OAuth support).

providerExtensions.name: Three Installation Scopes (local, project, user)
providerExtensions.summary: Three install scopes: local (~/.claude.json per-project, default; was 'project' in older CC versions), project (.mcp.json shareable with team), user (~/.claude.json global across projects; was 'global' in older CC versions). Scope precedence: local > project > user > plugin-provided > claude.ai connectors. The workspace server name is reserved and skipped at load time.

providerExtensions.name: OAuth 2.0 Authentication for Remote Servers
providerExtensions.summary: OAuth 2.0 for HTTP servers: dynamic client registration or CIMD auto-discovery; pre-configured credentials via --client-id/--client-secret; fixed callback port via --callback-port or oauth.callbackPort; oauth.scopes pins requested scope set (space-separated string per RFC 6749 §3.3; offline_access appended automatically if server advertises it); oauth.authServerMetadataUrl overrides discovery chain; token storage in keychain/credentials file with auto-refresh; MCP_CLIENT_SECRET env var for scripted credential supply.

providerExtensions.name: Environment Variable Expansion in .mcp.json
providerExtensions.summary: ${VAR} and ${VAR:-default} expansion in command, args, env, url, and headers fields of .mcp.json. Missing required variables with no default cause a config parse failure.

providerExtensions.name: MCP Tool Search (Deferred Tool Loading)
providerExtensions.summary: MCP tool schemas are deferred via ToolSearch; controlled by ENABLE_TOOL_SEARCH (unset=default defer, true=force defer including Vertex/non-first-party, auto=threshold 10%, auto:N=custom %, false=load all upfront); requires Sonnet 4+ or Opus 4+; Haiku models do not support tool search; disabled by default on Vertex AI and when ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL points to non-first-party host. Per-server alwaysLoad: true (CC v2.1.121+) exempts a server from deferral and blocks startup until connected. Per-tool _meta anthropic/alwaysLoad: true does same for individual tools.

providerExtensions.name: MCP Resources via @-Mention
providerExtensions.summary: MCP resources referenced via @server:protocol://path syntax; appear in @ autocomplete and auto-fetched when included. Resource paths are fuzzy-searchable.

providerExtensions.name: MCP Prompts as Slash Commands
providerExtensions.summary: MCP server prompts surface as /mcp__<servername>__<promptname> slash commands with space-separated arguments. Server and prompt names are normalized; spaces become underscores.

providerExtensions.name: MCP Elicitation (Mid-Task User Input)
providerExtensions.summary: MCP servers request mid-task user input via elicitation protocol (form or URL mode); interceptable by Elicitation hook events.

providerExtensions.name: MCP Output Token Limits and Per-Tool Annotation
providerExtensions.summary: MAX_MCP_OUTPUT_TOKENS (default 25k) caps tool output; warning displayed at 10k tokens. Per-tool _meta anthropic/maxResultSizeChars raises text limit up to 500k chars for that tool; applies independently of MAX_MCP_OUTPUT_TOKENS for text content. Image data still subject to MAX_MCP_OUTPUT_TOKENS. Oversized results spill to file.

providerExtensions.name: Managed MCP Configuration for Organizations
providerExtensions.summary: Enterprise control via managed-mcp.json (exclusive — users cannot add or modify servers) or allowedMcpServers/deniedMcpServers (policy — users can add servers within constraints) keyed by serverName, serverCommand, or serverUrl.

providerExtensions.name: Plugin-Bundled MCP Servers
providerExtensions.summary: Plugins bundle MCP servers via .mcp.json or plugin.json mcpServers key; uses ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA} variables; reload with /reload-plugins during a session.

providerExtensions.name: Dynamic Tool Updates via list_changed Notifications
providerExtensions.summary: Claude Code honors MCP list_changed notifications, auto-refreshing available tools/prompts/resources without reconnecting.

providerExtensions.name: Claude Code as an MCP Server
providerExtensions.summary: claude mcp serve exposes Claude's internal tools (View, Edit, LS, etc.) over MCP for consumption by other clients.

providerExtensions.name: MCP Channels for Push Messages
providerExtensions.summary: MCP servers declaring claude/channel capability can push messages into a session (CI results, alerts, webhooks); opted in via --channels flag.

providerExtensions.name: Import MCP Servers from Claude Desktop
providerExtensions.summary: claude mcp add-from-claude-desktop interactively imports server configs from Claude Desktop (macOS and WSL only).

providerExtensions.name: Dynamic Headers via headersHelper
providerExtensions.summary: headersHelper field specifies a shell command that runs at connection time (10-second timeout) and returns JSON headers; enables Kerberos, short-lived tokens, or custom SSO. Sets CLAUDE_CODE_MCP_SERVER_NAME and CLAUDE_CODE_MCP_SERVER_URL env vars. For project/local scope, runs only after workspace trust dialog acceptance.

providerExtensions.name: Automatic Reconnection with Exponential Backoff
providerExtensions.summary: HTTP and SSE servers reconnect automatically on disconnect: up to 5 attempts, starting at 1s, doubling each retry; initial connection retried up to 3 times for transient errors (CC v2.1.121+); authentication and not-found errors are not retried. stdio servers are not reconnected.

providerExtensions.name: Claude.ai Connector Servers
providerExtensions.summary: MCP servers configured in Claude.ai are automatically available in Claude Code when logged in with a Claude.ai account. Local server definitions take precedence over claude.ai connectors at the same endpoint. Disable via ENABLE_CLAUDEAI_MCP_SERVERS=false.
